hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — J, 9. Your opponent raised before the cards were dealt. You called.
Flop — King, 6, Queen. Your opponent bet. You called.
River — 5. Your opponent fired a large river bet.

wrongReasons: 2 6 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 2 6 would have.
3 2 has little reason to lead the river here — one pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
3 A has little reason to lead the river here — high card can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: K 5

Your opponent flopped two pair and bet all three streets with growing sizing. The consistent pressure — especially the larger turn bet — signals a hand strong enough to protect but not quite a monster.
